title: hieracium sagittatum subsp. exasciatum
intro: Hieracium sagittatum subsp. exasciatum, also known as the lesser hawk's beard, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae). It is traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can aid in the treatment of various health issues. The plant's medicinal properties are attributed to its high content of flavonoids, sesquiterpenes, and phenolic acids, which have been shown to possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects. Herbal preparations of Hieracium sagittatum subsp. exasciatum can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are used to alleviate symptoms of digestive issues, skin problems, and respiratory issues, as well as to boost the immune system.
